metadata.title:
EMPLOYMENT AGENCIES ARE


UP TO THEIR OLD TRICKS

pages.raw_text:
Are the employment agencies Of
the city taking an undue advantage
of the porer class of foreign labor
ers who apply to them for work !?


Judging from the number who
appeared at the mayors office yes
terday and asked for aid in
Strengthening out their troubles,
both employment agents and con-
tractors care little what treatment
Is accorded their patrons and en-
plOyes.


Early yesterday morning Gus
Prigan and Jake Bruntik, two Ger
mans, who spek little English and
read none at all, appeared at the
office of the city's executive and
told how they paid $3.50 and 81.
respectively, to the Inland Empire
Employment company for Jobs with
a lumber company. supposedly stu
ated west of this city.


/ Upon having the slips of paper
read to them by Garman friend,
the men discovered that instead of
going to the westward, the trans
portation called for trip to Blan-
chard. Idaho. As they inst came
here from that state the men were
anxious to go toward the coast. The
employment agent refused to help
them in any way and the mayors
office was resorted to.


At first the employment COM
pany refused point blank to do
anything for the men, but were
